_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-18 Pleural Decompression (Revised 2/13/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-18: Pleural Decompression — MDHHS Approved 2/13/2023
purpose: PARAMEDIC USE ONLY. Needle thoracostomy is indicated for SUSPECTED TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX (not simple pneumothorax) with hemodynamic compromise AND severe respiratory distress. Clinical diagnosis is required — do not delay for confirmatory imaging.

sections.title: Indications
points: PARAMEDIC USE ONLY
SUSPECTED TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX (NOT simple pneumothorax) with ALL of the following:
— Severe respiratory distress
— Hemodynamic compromise (hypotension and/or shock)
— Unilateral absent or significantly diminished breath sounds
ALSO CONSIDER in PEA arrest: after other reversible causes have been addressed and PEA persists — consider needle decompression bilaterally
Traumatic cardiac arrest: refer to Traumatic Arrest Protocol for guidance on needle decompression in arrest

sections.title: Clinical Presentation — Tension PTX
points: Severe respiratory distress PLUS hypotension
OR: difficult-to-ventilate hypotensive apneic patient with a confirmed correctly-positioned advanced airway (ETT or SGA)
Unilateral absent or diminished breath sounds
May have tracheal deviation (late finding — not required for diagnosis)
Distended neck veins (may be absent in hypovolemia)
Clinical diagnosis — do not wait for imaging confirmation in the prehospital setting

sections.title: Equipment
points: ADULT: needle minimum 14 gauge, minimum 3.5 inches in length, with NO flow-restricting valve (open system or one-way valve only)
PEDIATRIC: 18 gauge or 20 gauge needle; no flow-restricting valve
COMMERCIAL DEVICE: per MCA selection — separate MCA selection required for adult and pediatric commercial devices

sections.title: Insertion Sites
points: PREFERRED ADULT SITE: anterior axillary line, 4th intercostal space — insert just ABOVE the 5th rib (to avoid neurovascular bundle below the rib)
ALTERNATIVE ADULT SITE: midaxillary line, 4th intercostal space — insert just ABOVE the 5th rib
PREFERRED PEDIATRIC SITE: midclavicular line, 2nd intercostal space — insert just ABOVE the 3rd rib
CRITICAL: Always insert ABOVE the rib (neurovascular bundle runs along the inferior margin of each rib)
Insert perpendicular to chest wall; listen for rush of air upon entry
Secure needle/catheter in place; do not remove

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether an MCA-approved commercial needle thoracostomy device may be used in adult patients in addition to standard needle/catheter technique
options: Authorized — commercial device per MCA-approved brand/model

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether an MCA-approved commercial needle thoracostomy device may be used in pediatric patients
options: Authorized — commercial device per MCA-approved brand/model

deterioration_flags: No improvement after needle decompression — reassess breath sounds; consider bilateral decompression; check needle position and patency
Needle occlusion or kinking — repeat decompression at same or alternate site
Worsening hypotension after decompression — reassess; consider other reversible causes (hemorrhage, tamponade, tension on opposite side)
scope_notes: Indication: suspected TENSION pneumothorax (not simple PTX) with hemodynamic compromise + severe respiratory distress + unilateral absent/diminished breath sounds
Also consider in PEA after other reversible causes addressed
Adult needle: minimum 14 ga, minimum 3.5 inches, no flow-restricting valve
Pediatric needle: 18 ga or 20 ga, no flow-restricting valve
Preferred adult site: anterior axillary line, 4th ICS above 5th rib
Preferred pediatric site: midclavicular line, 2nd ICS above 3rd rib
ALWAYS insert above the rib — neurovascular bundle is below the rib
Commercial device per MCA selection (separate for adult and pediatric)
